plan_metadata.name: Feature DA Manifest File Function Manifest Plugin Support

steps.description: Select the "Declarative Agent – Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the "New Project" dropdown within Visual Studio Code.
Click the search input field in the "Add an action to your declarative agent" dropdown at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Type "Add an Action" into the search bar at the top of the "Create Declarative Agent" window in Visual Studio Code, triggering the contextual dropdown results.
Press Enter to select the "Add an Action" option from the dropdown in the "Create Declarative Agent" modal within Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Start with an OpenAPI Description Document" option in the "Create an Action" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter in the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location or Open File..." input field within the OpenAPI Spec Document dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter to confirm the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location" input in the VS Code Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.

Type the URL 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SLdragon/example-openapi-spec/main/real-no-auth.yaml' into the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL" input box in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension window in Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter to submit the OpenAPI Description Document URL in the input prompt at the top of the Visual Studio Code Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.

Click the search/filter input box in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" modal at the top of the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface, to activate text entry for filtering available API operations.
Press Enter to confirm selection in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" input field and activate the "OK" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default Folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
@code open json file #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/manifest.json, set value for child key "full" of key "description" to `"$[file('./instruction.txt')]"`
@code open json file #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/ai-plugin.json, set value for key "description_for_human" to a new value `"$[file('./instruction.txt')]"`
Click the "Provision" option under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the left sidebar of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.

Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code – Insiders, shown while viewing the "Overview of the Declarative Agent template" README with the "Provision" step selected in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar.
Type 'notifications: Show Notifications' into the VS Code command palette input at the top of the window.
Press Enter to select the "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
@code wait for #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/build/manifest.dev.json exist. Timeout: 120s
@assertion notification "provision stage executed successfully" exist.
Press F1 to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code while the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel is active.
Type text 'Notifications: Clear All Notifications' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Press Enter to select "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" from the command palette dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Explorer" icon (file tree) in the left sidebar of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "appPackage" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.
Click the "build" folder in the EXPLORER sidebar of Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.
Click the "manifest.dev.json" file under the "build" folder in the VS Code Explorer panel to open it.

Click the mouse cursor at the start of the "full" description field in the manifest.dev.json file, within Visual Studio Code, to place the text insertion point before the text "are a declarative agent created with Microsoft".
@assertion the value is starting with "You are a declarative agent"
Click the "ai-plugin.dev.json" file in the Explorer sidebar of Visual Studio Code to open it.
Click on the JSON text within the ai-plugin.dev.json file editor in Visual Studio Code, specifically at the beginning of the "description_for_human" value in the manifest section.
@assertion the value is starting with "You are a declarative agent"
